Six Charged In Connection To Shooting Incident

The Iredell County Sheriff’s Office has arrested six suspects, including one juvenile charged as an adult, after a dispute escalated into a shooting at a Statesville home. Sheriff Darren Campbell says deputies responded Thursday night to the 300 block of Branchwood Road after reports of shots fired into an occupied residence. Investigators say the suspects fled in a white BMW before deputies stopped the vehicle near Nixon and Shumaker Roads. Detectives determined the shooting stemmed from an earlier altercation at a downtown Statesville business. A search of the suspect vehicle uncovered a pistol and shell casings, while investigators confirmed the home…

Hickory Assistant City Manager Steve Miller Receives State Public Works Award

A longtime City of Hickory leader is being recognized for more than three decades of public service. Assistant City Manager Steve Miller received the Samuel A. Greeley Local Government Service Award from the North Carolina Chapter of the American Public Works Association during Monday night’s Hickory City Council meeting. The award honors public works professionals who have dedicated at least 30 years of service to a single local government agency while also contributing to the advancement of the public works profession. The performance starts at 7 p.m. on Friday, May 22, at the Hickory Community Theatre in downtown Hickory. Bookish bite. Sharp harmonies. Songs about saints, scientists, and stubborn women—this band delivers a sound that’s as fierce as it is thoughtful. Summer Safety Urged By NCDHHS

The North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services is urging residents to stay safe as summer activities increase across the state. Officials say water safety is critical during National Water Safety Month, noting that drowning remains a leading cause of death for young children. Parents are encouraged to actively supervise children near pools, lakes, and rivers, secure pool areas, and be aware of changing water conditions. State health leaders also warn of heat-related illness risks after thousands of emergency department visits last year. Symptoms such as dizziness, confusion, and nausea should be…

N.C. “Click It Or Ticket” Initiative Underway

The N.C. Governor’s Highway Safety Program announced that the annual Memorial Day “Click It Or Ticket” Campaign is underway. The initiative is in effect across North Carolina with the goal of promoting safe travel over the holiday weekend. Click It Or Ticket runs until May 31. The North Carolina Department of Transportation (NCDOT) says the initiative aims to raise public awareness through increased law enforcement patrols to encourage proper seat belt use and prevent deaths. Local law enforcement agencies across the state take part in the effort. In addition, to minimize traffic congestion during the heavy holiday travel volume, NCDOT pauses…

Lenoir Proposed Budget Keeps Tax Rate Unchanged, Raises Water And Sewer Rates

LENOIR, N.C. — Lenoir City Manager Scott Hildebran has proposed a $41,873,375 budget for the 2026-2027 fiscal year that would keep the city property tax rate unchanged while slightly increasing water and sewer rates. The proposal keeps the Lenoir property tax rate at 46 cents per $100 of value. The Rescue Readiness Tax would remain at 0.85 cents per $100, bringing the total city tax rate to 46.85 cents per $100. Catawba Valley Health System Opens New Community Pharmacy In Hickory

HICKORY, N.C. — Catawba Valley Health System has opened a new community pharmacy in Hickory, expanding healthcare and prescription services for patients and residents in the area. Health system officials announced the opening this week and recently held a ribbon-cutting ceremony at the new pharmacy location on the Catawba Valley Health System campus on Fairgrove Church Road Southeast. NASCAR Driver Kyle Busch Passes Away

Former NASCAR Cup Series Champion Kyle Busch passed away earlier today (Thursday). Busch was scheduled to race in the Legends series at Hickory Motor Speedway on Thursday as part of the ASA Stars National Tour. Instead, WHKY learned Thursday morning that he had fallen ill as he was unable to have a scheduled call in on WHKY’s On Second Thought program. Wittenburg Swim Beach Opens Saturday for Summer Season

The Wittenburg Access Swim Beach in Bethlehem opens for the summer Saturday, May 23. The beach will operate from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m., six days a week through Labor Day, closing Wednesdays for maintenance. Opening day will feature food trucks including Paw Paw’s Smash Burgers and Rita’s Ice, while Char Fresh Grill will be on-site Sunday. Visitors will also have access to concessions, picnic shelters, fishing areas, boat access, walking trails, and updated amenities at the revamped recreation area. Wildlife Officials Urge Bear Safety After Charlotte Sighting

North Carolina wildlife officials are reminding residents not to panic after a black bear was spotted in Charlotte’s Highland Creek neighborhood. The N.C. Wildlife Resources Commission says a resident captured video of the bear eating from a bird feeder Tuesday night. Officials say bear sightings increase across the Piedmont this time of year, especially between Memorial Day and early summer, as young bears travel in search of territory. Wildlife biologists say transient black bears have been seen in every county in North Carolina, including major cities like Charlotte and Durham. Hickory firefighters responded to a fire Tuesday afternoon (May 19) near the historic Ridgeview Cemetery. According to the Hickory Fire Department, at approximately 4:20 p.m. Tuesday, Engine 1 was dispatched to a reported grass fire in a near the 400 block of Highway 70 near the cemetery. Crews on the scene quickly upgraded the incident to a large woods fire, bringing additional engines and personnel to the scene. BBB: College Graduates Should Watch for Student Loan and Rental Scams

As college graduates across the country prepare to enter the workforce and begin life after campus, consumer advocates are warning that scammers are increasingly targeting young adults during one of the biggest transitions of their lives. From fake student loan forgiveness offers to fraudulent job postings and rental scams, experts say recent graduates are especially vulnerable as they navigate unfamiliar financial and personal responsibilities for the first time. Graduation season often brings major life changes, including moving to a new city, searching for a job, signing a lease and preparing to repay student loans. Scammers frequently use those milestones to exploit…

AAA is offering free Tow to Go rides during Memorial Day weekend in North Carolina. The program helps impaired drivers get home safely with their vehicle. AAA encourages anyone planning to drink or use impairing substances to also plan for a safe ride home. AAA Predicts Record Memorial Day Travel In North Carolina

AAA expects a record number of North Carolinians to travel this Memorial Day weekend. More than 1.2 million people in the state are expected to drive. AAA projects more than 1.3 million North Carolinians will travel at least 50 miles from home during the Memorial Day holiday period, setting a new state record. Alexander County Manager Presents Proposed 2026-2027 Budget

Alexander County leaders are looking at a proposed budget that would lower the county tax rate for the next fiscal year. County Manager Justin Mundy presented the proposed 2026-2027 budget during Monday night’s Board of Commissioners meeting. The proposed general fund budget totals nearly $62.8 million, which is about a 3.6 percent increase over the current year. The proposal would lower the county tax rate to 63 cents per $100 valuation. NC Launches Statewide Domestic Violence Fatality Review Collective

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson and the North Carolina Department of Justice are launching a statewide effort aimed at preventing domestic violence deaths by bringing together local fatality review teams from across the state. Currently, five North Carolina counties are authorized to conduct domestic violence fatality reviews. The new statewide collective will allow those teams to share data, identify patterns, and develop stronger recommendations to help prevent future violence. State officials say 13 people have died in domestic violence-related homicides and suicides in North Carolina so far this year. North Carolina Observes Heat Safety Week As Officials Warn Of Summer Dangers

Governor Josh Stein has proclaimed May 18-22 as Heat Safety Week in North Carolina to raise awareness about the health dangers posed by extreme temperatures and to highlight programs designed to help protect residents during the summer months. North Carolina’s official heat season runs from May 1 through September 30 each year. State officials say heat-related illness remains a growing concern across North Carolina. Crews arrived to find a skid steer fully engulfed in flames with nearby trees beginning to catch fire. Firefighters were able to quickly bring the blaze under control and prevent it from spreading deeper into the woods. State Health Officials Warn Residents To Protect Against Tick & Mosquito Illnesses

As temperatures rise across North Carolina, state health officials are warning residents to protect themselves from tick- and mosquito-borne illnesses. The North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services says more than 1,000 cases were reported statewide in 2025. May is Tick and Mosquito Awareness Month, and the state’s “Fight the Bite” campaign is encouraging people to use insect repellents, wear protective clothing and check for ticks after spending time outdoors. Health officials say Lyme disease, ehrlichiosis and spotted fever rickettsiosis are among the most common tick-borne illnesses in the state. According to documents, the Animal Welfare Section (AWS) received a complaint on April 8, 2026, alleging the shelter failed to provide adequate veterinary care before the dog’s death. The complaint prompted a formal investigation into the shelter’s handling of Lainey’s medical condition. Attorney General Warns Citizens Of Continuing NCDMV Scam

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning residents about a recurring text message scam involving criminals posing as the North Carolina Division of Motor Vehicles. According to the Attorney General’s Office, scammers are sending fake text messages claiming recipients have unpaid traffic citations and demanding immediate payment. The messages often threaten to suspend a driver’s license or cancel vehicle registration if payment is not made right away. “This scam is not new, and we’re seeing criminals continue to use it to take advantage of North Carolinians,” Jackson said. “If you get this text, delete it and block the sender. North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson has joined a bipartisan coalition of 25 attorneys general calling on major credit card companies and payment processors to crack down on illegal online vape sales, especially sales to minors. Hickory Fire Department crews responded to a trail rescue Tuesday morning (May 12) after a bicyclist was injured on the Broyhill Trail in northwest Hickory. According to officials, firefighters were dispatched shortly before 11 a.m. to the trail rescue call. Crews from Engine 3, Ladder 3, Rescue 1, and Battalion 1 responded to the scene. Firefighters located an injured bicyclist who had reportedly fallen along the trail. Crews provided medical care at the scene before using a motorized stretcher and the department’s UTV to safely evacuate the patient from the trail. Despite Recent Rainfall, Drought Continues In Hickory Area

The City of Hickory says drought conditions still require mandatory restrictions. Officials say recent rainfall was not enough to improve reservoir levels and inflow rates. City officials say reservoir levels, inflow rates, and drought conditions across the Catawba-Wateree River Basin have not improved enough to lift restrictions. Juneteenth Celebrations Scheduled Across Catawba County

Catawba County agencies and organizations are preparing to celebrate Juneteenth with a month-long series of events honoring freedom, history, and unity. Juneteenth—short for June Nineteenth—marks the day in 1865 when federal troops arrived in Galveston, Texas, announcing that enslaved people were free… more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed. The day commemorates the end of slavery in the United States and officially became a federal holiday in 2021. Throughout June, local agencies and organizations will host celebrations and educational events across the county, including live music, community gatherings, book discussions, storytelling programs, line dancing, worship celebrations, and festivals.…

NC to Receive $150 Million in Purdue Opioid Settlement

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson says a $7.4 billion settlement with Purdue Pharma and the Sackler family is now officially in effect, with North Carolina expected to receive nearly $150 million for addiction treatment, prevention, and recovery. Jackson says Purdue and the Sacklers built a business by fueling opioid addiction and knew the harm it was causing. He called the settlement the right outcome after years of legal battles. The agreement permanently bars the Sackler family from selling opioids in the United States. It also requires more than 30 million internal company documents tied to Purdue’s opioid business to be…

NC Wins $11 Million EpiPen Price Settlement Against Mylan

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson announced an $11 million settlement with drug maker Mylan, resolving allegations that the company used anti-competitive tactics to keep EpiPen prices artificially high and limit competition. Officials say a two-pack of EpiPens cost about $100 in 2007, but by 2016 the price had climbed to roughly $600. Under the settlement, the State Health Plan and North Carolina Medicaid will each receive about $4.2 million. State Treasurer Brad Briner called the overcharges “unconscionable,” saying the recovery helps protect taxpayers and state employees. Health experts also stressed that epinephrine auto-injectors are lifesaving medications, especially for patients with…

Burn Ban Lifted For Hickory Metro Counties

The N.C. Forest Service has lifted its statewide ban on open burning for 81 counties, effective 8 a.m. Sunday, May 3, following recent rainfall that helped reduce wildfire risk. The counties include Catawba, Alexander, Burke, Caldwell, Lincoln and Watauga. The ban had been in place since March 28 due to hazardous fire conditions. However, restrictions remain in 19 counties, including Iredell, where drought conditions persist. Agriculture Commissioner Steve Troxler says the rainfall has provided some relief, but warns the state is still in the peak of spring wildfire season. He urges residents to use extreme caution, noting conditions can change quickly.…

NC Names ROOTS Hub Leaders to Expand Rural Health Access

The North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services has announced the selection of organizations that will lead the state’s Rural Organizations Orchestrating Transformation for Sustainability, or ROOTS, Hubs under the Rural Health Transformation Program. State officials say the initiative will build regional networks connecting medical, behavioral health, and social services to improve access to care across all 100 counties, especially in rural communities. Selected hub leads include Impact Health for Region 1, Trillium Health Resources for Regions 2 and 5, Vaya Health for Region 3, UNC Hospitals for Region 4, and Access East for Region 6. NC Attorney General Warns of Rising Job Scams Targeting Workers

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning residents about a rise in employment scams targeting job seekers across the state. Jackson says scammers are posing as recruiters, posting fake jobs online, and conducting fraudulent interviews to steal money or personal information. Officials say the scams often promise high pay, little experience, or work-from-home opportunities. Victims may go through what looks like a real hiring process before being asked for Social Security numbers, bank information, or payments for training, equipment, or background checks. Jackson says job seekers should be cautious if an offer seems too good to be true or if…

NCDOL Announces Workplace Safety Policy Change

North Carolina Labor Commissioner Luke Farley has announced a major workplace safety policy change aimed at increasing accountability after on-the-job deaths. The N.C. Department of Labor says it will immediately stop reducing penalties in fatal workplace safety cases based on the size of a business. For decades, the state followed a federal OSHA-style policy that sometimes cut fines by as much as 70 percent in cases involving worker deaths — a practice critics called the “death discount.” Farley says when a worker dies because of safety failures, the response should fully reflect the seriousness of that loss. State labor officials say…

Hickory, Surrounding Communities Enact Mandatory Water Limits Under Stage Two Drought

According to City of Hickory officials, due to ongoing extremely dry conditions across the region, the Catawba-Wateree Drought Management Advisory Group (CW‑DMAG) has declared that the Catawba-Wateree River Basin has entered Stage 2 of the Low Inflow Protocol (LIP). In accordance with the City of Hickory’s Water Shortage Response Plan, Hickory has officially entered Stage 2, triggering mandatory water use restrictions for all customers. Surrounding communities have also enacted water restrictions. Surrounding communities and Catawba County have also enacted restrictions. The North Carolina State Board of Elections says it has identified approximately 34,000 deceased individuals still listed on the state’s voter rolls after a large-scale data comparison with the federal SAVE database. On April 17, the board submitted more than 7.3 million voter records as part of an ongoing effort to verify citizenship status and improve the accuracy of voter registration lists. Frye Regional Announces New Heart Procedure

Frye Regional Medical Center has become the first hospital in western North Carolina to perform an FDA-approved implantable neuromodulation procedure for heart failure patients. The milestone expands advanced treatment options available through the Frye Regional Heart Center. The minimally invasive procedure was performed by Dr. Brett Starr. It uses gentle electrical signals delivered to receptors in the carotid artery to help regulate heart rate, blood pressure, and fluid balance for patients still experiencing symptoms despite standard treatments. Unlike pacemakers or defibrillators, the device does not place hardware inside the heart. It is implanted under the skin in the chest, and most…

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning residents about a rise in investment scams spreading across Meta platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. Scammers are using deepfake videos and fake celebrity endorsements to lure victims into cryptocurrency fraud, pump-and-dump schemes, and fake investment platforms. Officials say red flags include promises of guaranteed returns, pressure to act fast, and requests to move conversations to encrypted apps like WhatsApp or Telegram. The Golden LEAF Foundation was established in 1999 to receive a portion of North Carolina’s funding from the 1998 Master Settlement Agreement with cigarette manufacturers. City of Hickory Offers Recycling Tips

The City of Hickory is offering recycling tips. Doing your part to recycle helps keep valuable materials out of the landfill, but only when the right items go into the recycling bin. Three of the most common contaminants that cause big problems in the recycling process are plastic bags, batteries, and biological waste. Keeping these items out of your recycling rollout protects the entire system and ensures your efforts truly make a difference. Burke Officials Remind Citizens That Burn Ban Continues

The Burke County Joint Information Center issued a news release today (April 27) stating that, despite the rain this weekend, it was not enough to lift the current burn ban. At this time, burning remains prohibited on all state, federal and private property. This burn ban is being strictly enforced across Burke County. Authorities ask that you save yourself time, money and potential penalties by not burning until we receive widespread, meaningful rainfall and the ban is officially lifted. State Officials Fighting Hidden Fees Imposed On Renters

North Carolina’s Attorney General is joining a bipartisan effort to eliminate hidden rental fees. Jeff Jackson and attorneys general from more than two dozen states are urging the Federal Trade Commission to require landlords to clearly disclose total rent costs upfront. The proposal would ban so-called junk fees that can raise costs after a lease is signed. Officials say about 40 percent of renters in North Carolina already spend at least 35 percent of their income on housing. The FTC is now reviewing public input on the proposal.

Officials say criminals are posing as recruiters, posting fake job listings online, and even conducting what appear to be real interviews. Victims may receive offer letters and onboarding paperwork before scammers ask for Social Security numbers, bank details, or upfront payments for training, equipment, or background checks. Free Summer Concert Series in Downtown Morganton

Get ready to kick off your weekends with the sound of summer! The highly anticipated 2026 TGIF Summer Concert Series Presented by Case Farms is returning to downtown with an exciting and diverse lineup of live music. From soulful rhythm and blues to classic rock favorites and country hits, there’s something for everyone to enjoy under the stars. This free concert series takes place every Friday evening in May, June, and July (except for July 4), transforming downtown into a lively gathering place filled with music, community, and summer fun. Caldwell County Road Closure Announced

N.C. Department of Transportation crews will install a detour on a Caldwell County road today (April 23).  U.S. 64/N.C. 90 will close between Fox Road and Station Road from 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. while investigators from the North Carolina State Highway Patrol reconstruct a crash from over the weekend. The detour will guide traffic to Fox Road then Draco Road then Union Baptist Road then Station Road before returning to U.S. 64/N.C. 90. NCDOT crews will be posted at each intersection to direct traffic.  For real-time travel information, visit DriveNC.gov or follow NCDOT on social media.

Authorities Warn About Dangerous Drug

The Burke County Sheriff’s Office is alerting the public about a dangerous synthetic opioid known as cychlorphine. Officials say the drug can be 10 to 20 times more potent than fentanyl and has been linked to overdose deaths nationwide. It’s often mixed with other drugs, increasing the risk for users who may not know what they’re taking. While not yet confirmed locally, authorities warn it could already be in the area. Residents are urged to avoid unknown substances and call 911 in the event of a suspected overdose.

Read more

Document Destruction Day Scheduled In Alexander County

Alexander County residents have an opportunity to protect themselves from identity theft next month. The Public Works and Facilities Department will host a free Document Destruction Day on Saturday, May 2, from 8 a.m. until noon. The event will take place at the county administration building parking lot on Liledoun Road in Taylorsville. Residents can safely dispose of sensitive documents containing personal information. Officials say shredding documents is an important step in preventing fraud.

Read more

Phone Scam Warning Issued By Burke Sheriff’s Office

The Burke County Sheriff’s Office is warning residents about a phone scam involving callers posing as law enforcement. Officials say scammers are spoofing the sheriff’s office number and claiming victims missed a federal grand jury duty. Victims are told they can avoid arrest by paying a fine, often through Bitcoin machines. Authorities stress law enforcement will never demand payment over the phone or threaten immediate arrest. Residents are urged to hang up on suspicious calls and contact law enforcement directly.

Read more

NCDOT To Promote Bicycle Safety Through Distribution Of Helmets

The North Carolina Department of Transportation is expanding efforts to improve bicycle safety across the state. The agency’s Integrated Mobility Division has announced 184 organizations will receive helmets through the 2026 Bicycle Helmet Initiative. Recipients include schools, local governments, nonprofits, and public health agencies working to promote safe riding. The program provides helmets, safety education, and community outreach to help reduce injuries and save lives. For a full list of recipients, visit the NCDOT website.

Catawba County Commissioners Vote To Merge School Systems

The Catawba County Board of Commissioners public hearing during which speakers are being given a chance to speak for or against the proposed merger of the three Catawba County school systems wrapped up at 11:12 p.m. Monday (April 20). Dozens of speakers representing various factions in the community and from the county’s systems have addressed the board, with most either strongly opposing the merger, or asking commissioners to at least take more time before deciding. NCDOJ & Attorney General Warn Citizens About Data Breaches

North Carolina is seeing a surge in data breaches, with a record number reported last year—and now, state officials are warning of a new cybersecurity threat that could put even more personal information at risk. According to the North Carolina Department of Justice, businesses and government agencies reported 2,349 data breaches in 2025, the highest number ever recorded in the state. Those breaches exposed the personal information of nearly 9.3 million North Carolinians. NC Officials Urge Workers To Take Precautions In Heat

As temperatures across North Carolina climb into the upper 80s and low 90s this week, the NC Department of Labor is urging workers to take proactive steps to protect themselves from heat-related illnesses. Heat-related illnesses, including heat exhaustion and heat stroke, can develop quickly in high temperatures, especially for workers in outdoor industries such as construction, agriculture, and landscaping, as well as those in non-climate-controlled indoor environments. NCDOL emphasizes three key prevention strategies: Rest, water and shade. The Department of Labor also reminds everyone to recognize the signs of heat stress and to respond quickly if symptoms arise. Frye Reaches Stroke Reduction Procedure Milestone

Frye Regional Medical Center has reached a significant milestone by completing more than 500 minimally invasive implant procedures designed to help reduce stroke risk for individuals living with nonvalvular atrial fibrillation (AFib). The procedure uses an FDA-approved device for carefully selected patients with nonvalvular atrial fibrillation who meet specific clinical criteria. Candidates are evaluated by a multidisciplinary heart team and typically include individuals at increased risk of stroke who are unable to tolerate long-term oral anticoagulation due to bleeding risk or other medical considerations. There was no fire or damage, just a faulty detector. Alarms are treated as an actual fire until proven otherwise, are fully investigated prior to departing the scene. Attorney General Jeff Jackson Calls on Congress to Pass Bipartisan Legislation That Would Regulate Deadly Drug Xylazine

Attorney General Jeff Jackson on March 31 joined a bipartisan group of attorneys general urging Congress to pass the Combating Illicit Xylazine Act (S. 545/ H.R. 1266). The bipartisan bill would classify xylazine as a Schedule III substance under federal law, which would help prevent the illicit use of the dangerous drug and ultimately save lives. Xylazine, also called tranq, is a veterinary tranquilizer linked to overdose deaths in the United States. It is not approved for human use but is increasingly being mixed with illicit opioids, such as fentanyl, to increase their street value or make their effects more potent.…

The oral rabies vaccination program helps prevent the raccoon rabies from moving west of the Appalachian Mountains, where raccoon rabies does not exist. Additional baits will be distributed by helicopter and by hand in the Asheville area from April 13-16. Spring Litter Sweep Scheduled Later This Month

The 2026 Spring Litter Sweep is taking place April 11-25 . Traditionally scheduled for the last two weeks of April and September, Litter Sweep is the N.C. Department of Transportation’s biannual statewide roadside litter removal initiative. Residents throughout the state participate in local efforts to help clean up North Carolina’s roadways. In addition to volunteers, NCDOT maintenance crews devote one week of their time to pick up litter and collect orange bags placed on the roadsides by volunteer pickups. Attorney General Warns Veterans About Scam

Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning veterans across North Carolina about a scam that offers veterans monthly payments and free dental coverage through a Veteran Savings Program. If people call the number listed, scammers will ask for their personal and financial data. “Veterans deserve our respect and support, not scams that try to take advantage of their service,” said Attorney General Jeff Jackson. “If it sounds like someone is offering you free money – it’s probably a scam. Statewide Burn Ban Implemented

Due to increased wildfire risk, the N.C. Forest Service has issued a ban on all open burning and has canceled all burning permits statewide effective 6 p.m. Saturday, March 28 until further notice.   Under North Carolina law, the ban prohibits all open burning statewide, regardless of whether a permit was previously issued. The issuance of any new permits has also been suspended until the ban is lifted. Anyone violating the burn ban faces a $100 fine plus $183 court costs. Wastewater Discharge Reported In Hickory

The City of Hickory had a discharge of untreated wastewater on Thursday (March 26) of an estimated 4,500 gallons at 1016 Old Lenoir Rd NW. During preventive maintenance on the sewer main, foreign debris in the line created a blockage. Approximately 4,000 gallons of untreated wastewater were discharged into Frye Creek, in the Catawba River Basin. Governor Josh Stein announced a statewide expansion of Law Enforcement Assisted Diversion, or LEAD, programs aimed at helping people struggling with addiction get treatment instead of facing jail time. The initiative, led by the North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services and the North Carolina Harm Reduction Coalition, connects people charged with low-level, non-violent offenses to mental health, medical, and social services. No additional information was released regarding this incident.

NCDOR Warns of Scam Texts Targeting Tax Refunds

The North Carolina Department of Revenue (NCDOR) is alerting residents to a growing scam involving fraudulent text messages that claim to be from the agency regarding tax refunds. According to officials, scammers are sending messages that appear legitimate, often referencing “refund processing” or requesting “bank account information.” These texts frequently include urgent language and threats—such as possible “forfeiture” of a refund—to pressure recipients into acting quickly. Authorities say the goal is to trick individuals into clicking malicious links that can steal sensitive personal and financial information, potentially leading to identity theft. NCDOR emphasized that it does not contact taxpayers through text…

Increased Fire Danger Warning Issued

The National Weather Service issued a special weather statement Sunday afternoon (March 22). There is an increased fire danger from Monday afternoon (March 23) through Monday evening in several western North Carolina counties including those in the Hickory Metro. . Dry brush and leaf litter have become very dry in recent days due to breezy conditions and low relative humidity. These conditions are forecast again tomorrow. The attorneys general filed a brief to protect a Safe Drinking Water Act rule that requires public water systems to replace lead service lines. North Carolina’s Injury Rate For Mine & Quarry Workers Declines

North Carolina Labor Commissioner Luke Farley recently announced that the state’s non-fatal injury rate for mine and quarry workers has declined even further, dropping from 0.82 to 0.68 per 200,000 man hours since September. The new rate is less than half the current national average of 1.38. In addition, the rate of incidents involving fatal injuries has also reduced, falling from 0.358 to 0.270 per 200,000 man hours over the same period. Law Enforcement Agency Issues Scam Warning

The Caldwell County Sheriff’s Office is warning citizens about an ongoing scam known as the “Grandparent Scam”. The department has received reports of older citizens getting phone calls from someone on behalf of their grandchild, or claiming to actually be their grandchild. The caller says the grandchild has been arrested and urgently needs money to get out of jail. Sometimes another person gets on the phone pretending to be an attorney or law enforcement officer, demanding bond money be sent immediately through cash apps, gift cards, wire transfers, or even cash pickups. Law enforcement will never call and demand money over…

Daylight Saving Time Begins This Weekend, “Spring Forward” Sunday Morning

It’s time to “spring forward” once again, we move into daylight saving time this weekend. Daylight saving time begins at 2 a.m. Sunday (March 8) and ends Sunday, November 1 at 2 a.m. Set any manual clocks you have ahead one hour when you go to bed Saturday night. Most devices including smartphones automatically make the switch. This will result in one less hour of sleep Saturday night into Sunday morning, but will add an hour of daylight in the evenings.

Hickory City Council Approves Updates To Strengthen Sign Regulations

Hickory City Council approved updates to the City’s Land Development Code to strengthen enforcement of sign regulations. Under the new rules, repeat violations will result in immediate fines. The first violation will normally result in a warning notice, with 10 days to correct the violation without receiving a fine. After that, any repeat offense at the same property within two years will not receive another warning—you will be fined immediately. This applies to a variety of signage types, including yard signs, feather flags, inflatables, and similar displays. The full Land Development Code can be viewed online at www.hickorync.gov/land-development-code. If you have…

The Hickory Police Department’s March traffic enforcement P.A.C.T. – Pay Attention in City Traffic – will be located on Hwy 127 South from 1st Avenue SE to 4th Street Drive SW. Enforcement will focus on red-light violations, distracted driving, speeding, and other traffic violations. Pedestrians are encouraged to cross safely; use crosswalks, cross at the corner or other designated crossing areas; obey pedestrian signal indicators; and wear reflective materials when walking at night. N.C. Labor Commissioner Announces Safety Initiative

Labor Commissioner Luke Farley announced the implementation of a new workplace safety initiative aimed at reducing struck-by injuries and fatalities in workplaces across North Carolina. The initiative will strategically target struck-by hazards in both general industry and construction. Struck-by incidents, which involve workers being hit by materials, tools, or equipment — too often result in serious injury or death. In response, NCDOL’s new workplace safety initiative strengthens efforts to strategically target and eliminate these preventable hazards. Attorney General Jeff Jackson Reaches $100 Million Multistate Settlement with Walmart for Deceiving Drivers and Customers Over Delivery

Attorney General Jeff Jackson and a bipartisan group of attorneys general, along with the Federal Trade Commission (FTC), reached a $100 million multistate settlement with Walmart over allegations that the company deceived customers and drivers who participated in its Spark Driver Program. Walmart will pay drivers in North Carolina approximately $2 million that they lost because of Walmart’s alleged actions. Walmart has run the Spark Driver program since 2018. Local Authorities Warn Of Scam Involving Pet Photos

According to the Caldwell County Sheriff’s Office, scammers are increasingly targeting people looking for pets online, especially puppies, by asking for a deposit up front, before the victim even sees the pet in person, then disappearing once the money is sent. Here’s what usually happens: Scammers post pet photos online, like Facebook, Craigslist, Instagram, or fake breeder websites. They claim the pet is in high demand and that multiple families are interested. They ask for a deposit to hold the pet, usually through Cash App, Zelle, Venmo, Apple Pay, or gift cards. Caldwell County is bidding farewell to its longtime Public Library Director, Leslie Griffin, who will retire at the end of this month after nearly a decade of service to the community. Lenoir-Rhyne University is offering a new course designed to help individuals and professionals work more effectively with artificial intelligence. The eight-week micro-credential program, Applied AI Foundations: Working Productively with AI, will begin March 3 and run through Mini Session Two of the spring 2026 term. The hands-on course, which requires no prerequisites, will be held on campus and focuses on applying AI to real-world tasks rather than technical theory. Participants will learn to use AI as a tool for productivity—building workflows, drafting content, and evaluating AI-generated outputs for accuracy, bias, and context. North Carolina is expanding a program that helps communities attract new businesses and jobs. The NC Selectsite Readiness Program will add five new locations in western North Carolina. N.C. Attorney General and Insurance Commissioner Put Pharmacy Benefit Managers on Notice Regarding New Drug Price Protections

Attorney General Jeff Jackson and Insurance Commissioner Mike Causey have alerted pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) that North Carolina’s SCRIPT Act is now in effect and the Department of Justice and Department of Insurance intend to work together to ensure fair drug prices. PBMs act as middlemen between drug manufacturers and health insurers. While they began as a way to streamline prescription drug claims for insurance companies, they now have immense power over people’s access to prescription drugs, the prices they pay for them, and how much pharmacies get reimbursed. A few PBMs control more than three-fourths of the country’s 6.6 billion…

A 2024 Ford Caldwell County ambulance was traveling East on NC 18, crossed the centerline and collided with a Westbound 2011 Toyota Rav4, in Caldwell County. Major Sewer Project Completed In Alexander County

The Alexander Industrial Park sewer project is officially complete, marking a major milestone in the county’s ongoing efforts to attract new business and support existing industry. County staff, engineers, and utility representatives gathered on January 26 for a final walk-through of the project, which includes approximately 2,440 feet of newly installed 8-inch gravity sewer line. Alexander Commissioners Celebrate Payoff Of Law Enforcement Center

The Alexander County Board of Commissioners held a ceremonial note-burning to celebrate the payoff of the Thomas E. Bebber Sr. Alexander County Law Enforcement Center, officially making the county’s general fund debt-free. The final payment was made in January 2026. The law enforcement center, located at 91 Commercial Park Avenue, opened in November 2012 following years of planning. The 50,000-square-foot facility includes approximately 10,000 square feet for the sheriff’s office and 40,000 square feet for the detention center, which can house up to 164 inmates. Man Charged In Drug-Related Death Extradited To Catawba County

A man indicted last month in connection to a fentanyl related death in Catawba County is now being held in a local jail following extradition. 26-year-old Juan Sebastian Solarte of Newton was indicted by a Catawba County Grand Jury for death by distribution and sale of schedule II controlled substance. On January 20, jury members returned an indictment charging Solarte in connection with the death of 26-year-old Jacob Lee Kirk. Kirk was found deceased at his residence in February 2025. An autopsy conducted by the North Carolina Office of the Chief Medical Examiner determined the cause of death to be fentanyl…

Funding Awarded To NCDPI To Strengthen Mental Health Services

The North Carolina Department of Public Instruction (NCDPI) has been awarded more than $11 million in federal funding to strengthen school-based mental health services, following a recent announcement from the U.S. Department of Education. The two grants received will support school psychology training, recruitment and retention efforts, with a focus on high-need and rural school districts. The funding is part of more than $208 million awarded nationwide through the Mental Health Service Professional Demonstration Program and the School-Based Mental Health Services Program. The Mental Health Service Professional Demonstration Grant will provide $4.8 million over four years for the NC School Psychology…

Catawba County Commissioners Meet, Approve Several Items

The Catawba County Board of Commissioners met in regular session Monday (February 16). Chief Financial Officer Mary Morrison presented the Fiscal Year 2024-25 Financial Audit along with Paula Hughes, CPA, of Martin Starnes and Associates. The board adopted the audit. A public hearing was held to consider a proposed Economic Development Agreement and related resolution between Catawba County and Kooima Company, commit to splitting the 5% ($2,500.00) Building Reuse Grant match with the City of Conover, and authorize the Chair to execute these along with any other needed documents. School Bus Struck In Alexander County

An Alexander County Schools Bus was struck this morning (February 17) on Highway 90 east in Taylorsville. According to our news partner WACB, Alexander County Schools Superintendent Dr Bill Griffin says the bus was enroute to Hiddenite Elementary. The bus was rear-ended by another vehicle. No students, nor the driver were injured. All were transferred to another bus and brought to school. [Story courtesy of Rick Gilbert, WACB]

Open House Scheduled At Frye Regional Lenoir Campus

Frye Regional Medical Center, a Duke LifePoint hospital, will host a community Open House on Monday, March 16, from 4:30 to 6 p.m. The event celebrates the opening of its new Frye Regional Imaging Center – Lenoir at Frye Regional HealthPark. Community members are invited to tour the facility, meet staff and learn about advanced imaging services now available closer to home. The Imaging Center is located at 1041 Morganton Blvd. SW in Lenoir. It expands Frye Regional’s services in Caldwell County and helps make care easier to access. The center represents a $3.5 million investment in the community and supports…

Autism Awareness Walk Scheduled In Hickory

To celebrate Autism Awareness Month, the City of Hickory Parks, Recreation & Sports Tourism Department is hosting the annual Autism Awareness Fun Walk at Bruce Meisner Park on Saturday, April 4, from 10 a.m. to 12 p.m. Participation in this event is free, but donations are accepted at https://tinyurl.com/autismawarenesswalk. All proceeds will be used to purchase sensory bags for Highland Recreation Center and benefit We Rock the Spectrum of Hickory. All who choose to donate will be entered into a raffle to win a prize. Wilson Creek Day Use Area Open For First Time Since Helene

The U.S. Forest Service announced the reopening of the Wilson Creek Gorge day use area. Officials say Hurricane Helene caused significant changes to the landscape, including the course of the river and fishing areas. This means there may be new hazards present. They say this will also give you the opportunity to find a new favorite fishing hole this spring. Additional recovery work will be ongoing in the area, and while officials invite you to enjoy the area and all that it has to offer, they ask that you recreate responsibly by following these rules while in the day use area:…

Brush Fires Fought In Alexander County

According to North Carolina Forest Service Ranger David Huffman, there were two significant brush fires on Friday (February 13) in Alexander County. The first burned four to five acres off Poplar Spring Road in the Hiddenite area. Multiple units and the NC Forest Service were able to contain the fire. Another fire consumed two to three acres of heavily wooded terrain in the Ellendale Community off McDonald Drive. An aircraft monitored both fires. Huffman said the wind, low humidity, and dry fuels atop the ground contributed to the fires. [Story and photo courtesy of Rick Gilbert, WACB]

Multiple Agencies Fight Grass Fire In Burke County

Chesterfield Fire Rescue volunteers responded along with Oak Hill Fire & Rescue Protection Association, Inc., Triple Community Fire Department, and Burke County EMS to a grass fire just off of Antioch Road Friday afternoon (February 13). The fire burned less than an acre, but quickly spread toward a nearby home. The agencies were able to stop the fire from spreading to the home and minimize damage to other outbuildings on site. Inmate Death In Burke County Under Investigation

The Burke County Sheriff’s Office reports the death of an inmate. On January 18, 2026, an inmate was found unresponsive in his cell at the Burke County Jail after another detainee alerted detention staff. Detention and medical personnel responded immediately and initiated life-saving measures while requesting assistance from Burke County EMS, who also responded to the scene. The inmate was transported to an area medical facility for medical treatment. After several weeks of medical care, he passed away on February 11. The Burke County Sheriff’s Office has requested that the North Carolina State Bureau of Investigation (SBI) conduct an independent investigation.…

Early 1900s Era Home Destroyed By Fire In Alexander County

Firefighters in Alexander County responded to a residential fire early this morning (February 13). The fire occurred on the 4400 Block of Rink Dam Road and destroyed the home. Firefighters from the Bethlehem, Wittenburg and Ellendale Fire Departments responded at approximately 1 a.m. today. The home was fully involved in fire upon arrival of firefighters. According to reports, one occupant was able to escape from the home to safety. Alexander County Fire Marshal Chris Hicks says that the house dates back to the early 1900s and it was a total loss. New Traffic Lights To Be Activated At Alexander County Intersection

The N.C. Department of Transportation will activate traffic signals at an Alexander County intersection next Monday, February 16. Crews installed signals at the N.C. 16 and Millersville Road intersection after development in the area led to increased traffic. Barry Cross, deputy division traffic engineer for Alexander and five other counties in the North Carolina foothills. said “Adding signals to this intersection will improve traffic flow and enhance safety on one of Alexander County’s major thoroughfares,” The signals, which crews installed earlier this week, will remain in a flashing mode until they are activated Monday morning. Attorney General Warns Citizens Of Continuing NCDMV Scam

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning residents about a recurring text message scam involving criminals posing as the North Carolina Division of Motor Vehicles. According to the Attorney General’s Office, scammers are sending fake text messages claiming recipients have unpaid traffic citations and demanding immediate payment. The messages often threaten to suspend a driver’s license or cancel vehicle registration if payment is not made right away. “This scam is not new, and we’re seeing criminals continue to use it to take advantage of North Carolinians,” Jackson said. “If you get this text, delete it and block the sender. State…

N.C. Attorney General Warns State Residents About Investment Scams

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning residents about a rise in investment scams spreading across Meta platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. Scammers are using deepfake videos and fake celebrity endorsements to lure victims into cryptocurrency fraud, pump-and-dump schemes, and fake investment platforms. Officials say red flags include promises of guaranteed returns, pressure to act fast, and requests to move conversations to encrypted apps like WhatsApp or Telegram. Jackson urges North Carolinians to research any investment opportunity carefully, verify credentials, and be cautious of crypto transactions, which are hard to recover once sent. Anyone who believes they’ve been targeted…

NC Officials Warn Of Employment Scams

North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson and Labor Commissioner Luke Farley are warning residents about a rise in employment scams targeting job seekers. Officials say criminals are posing as recruiters, posting fake job listings online, and even conducting what appear to be real interviews. Victims may receive offer letters and onboarding paperwork before scammers ask for Social Security numbers, bank details, or upfront payments for training, equipment, or background checks. State leaders say some scams also involve fake checks that later bounce, leaving victims responsible for the money. According to the Federal Trade Commission, Americans reported losing more than $500 million…

Jury Duty Scam Warning Issued

The Caldwell County Sheriff’s Office is warning residents about a jury duty scam making the rounds again. Authorities say scammers are calling people in Caldwell County and nearby areas pretending to be deputies with the sheriff’s office. Callers claim the victim missed jury duty and now has a federal warrant for their arrest. Investigators say the scammer then demands the person go to Walmart and buy a money card for a certain amount to avoid being arrested. Officials stress the calls are fraudulent and residents should not send money or provide personal information. Anyone receiving one of these calls is urged…

N.C. Insurance Commissioner Reaches Settlement On Dwelling Rates

North Carolina’s Insurance Commissioner has reached a settlement that will raise dwelling insurance rates—but far less than originally proposed. Commissioner Mike Causey announced an average increase of 5 percent per year over the next two years. That’s significantly lower than the nearly 70 percent increase requested by the N.C. Rate Bureau. The first increase takes effect October 2026, with another in October 2027. Officials say rates will vary by region. The agreement also includes discounts for fortified homes and eliminates the need for a planned hearing this summer.

Read more

Woman Arrested On Drug Charges After Being Found Unconscious In Vehicle

A Morganton woman is facing drug charges after police say she was found unconscious behind the wheel at a gas station. Officers responded Tuesday night and found 44-year-old Andrea Lavonne Allbritton passed out in the driver’s seat, holding suspected drug paraphernalia. A K-9 search of the vehicle uncovered what officers described as a significant amount of drugs. Allbritton is charged with trafficking fentanyl and multiple counts of possession with intent to sell or deliver. She was placed in the Burke County Jail without bond.

Authorities Warn About Dangerous Drug

The Burke County Sheriff’s Office is alerting the public about a dangerous synthetic opioid known as cychlorphine. Officials say the drug can be 10 to 20 times more potent than fentanyl and has been linked to overdose deaths nationwide. It’s often mixed with other drugs, increasing the risk for users who may not know what they’re taking. While not yet confirmed locally, authorities warn it could already be in the area. Residents are urged to avoid unknown substances and call 911 in the event of a suspected overdose.

Read more

Phone Scam Warning Issued By Burke Sheriff’s Office

The Burke County Sheriff’s Office is warning residents about a phone scam involving callers posing as law enforcement. Officials say scammers are spoofing the sheriff’s office number and claiming victims missed a federal grand jury duty. Victims are told they can avoid arrest by paying a fine, often through Bitcoin machines. Authorities stress law enforcement will never demand payment over the phone or threaten immediate arrest. Residents are urged to hang up on suspicious calls and contact law enforcement directly.

Read more

According to our news partner WACB, police say the victim’s husband, 61-year-old Eddie McCollum, called 911 and admitted to the shooting. Morganton Authorities Warn Of Scammers Requesting Payment For Warrants Or Fines

Authorities are continuing to warn residents about scams costing victims tens of thousands of dollars. Officials say scammers often pose as government agencies, demanding payment for warrants, fines, or missed obligations. According to a news release from the Morganton Department of Public Safety, the scammers may request money through gift cards, cryptocurrency, or cash pickups. Law enforcement stresses they will never demand payment this way. Residents are urged to verify any requests and look out for elderly neighbors who may be targeted.

Local Law Enforcement Agency Reports Court Date Scam

The Burke County Sheriff’s Office was recently made aware that members of the community have received scam phone calls. In these calls, the caller falsely claims that the individual has missed a court appearance and must immediately pay a fine before ending the call. The Sheriff’s Office says that legitimate law enforcement agencies and court officials will not request payment over the phone or demand immediate payment in this manner. If you receive a call like this, do not provide any personal or financial information and hang up immediately. Officials encourage everyone to remain vigilant and report any suspicious calls to…

Traffic Stop Leads to Arrest in Alexander County

On Friday, April 3rd, 2026, an officer with the Taylorsville Police Department observed a white pickup truck traveling southbound on NC-16. Attorney General Warns Veterans About Scam

Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning veterans across North Carolina about a scam that offers veterans monthly payments and free dental coverage through a Veteran Savings Program. No such program exists. The scam uses high-pressure tactics and urges recipients to “call within five days” for more information. If people call the number listed, scammers will ask for their personal and financial data. “Veterans deserve our respect and support, not scams that try to take advantage of their service,” said Attorney General Jeff Jackson. “If it sounds like someone is offering you free money – it’s probably a scam. Please report them to my office…

Attorney General Warns N.C. Residents Of Scam Targeting Drivers

Attorney General Jeff Jackson is warning North Carolinians of a new text scam targeting drivers with a fake parking and toll violation notice. The message claims the recipient has an outstanding parking or toll violation that has led to an enforcement action against them and demands that the person either pay immediately or risk severe legal consequences, including a court appearance, fines, or driver’s license suspension. The message looks very official. It references North Carolina laws, a case number, a court hearing date and location, and a non-existent judge and clerk of court – but it is fake. Fake “Notice Of Default” Circulating In Alexander County, Officials Warn

The Alexander County Clerk of Superior Court’s Office is warning residents about a fraudulent “Notice of Default” that is circulating and falsely appears to be an official North Carolina court document. Officials stress the document is not legitimate and is part of an ongoing scam designed to trick people into sending money or sharing personal information. According to the Clerk’s Office, the fake notices often look convincing and may include official-looking seals, court-style formatting, and references to fabricated case numbers or statutes. The documents may also claim urgent enforcement action and instruct recipients to scan a QR code or make an…

Scam Warning Issued

Residents are being urged to stay alert after reports of a new scam circulating in the area. Caldwell County Sheriff Kevin Bean says his office has received multiple calls from concerned citizens about individuals impersonating representatives from the state Department of Motor Vehicles. The scammers are contacting people by phone and text, claiming victims owe unpaid tolls, traffic tickets, or registration fees. According to the sheriff, the messages often appear urgent and threaten consequences such as driver’s license suspension or legal action. Victims are then instructed to click a link or provide payment information—both of which are part of the scam.…

Law Enforcement Agency Issues Scam Warning

The Caldwell County Sheriff’s Office is warning citizens about an ongoing scam known as the “Grandparent Scam”. The department has received reports of older citizens getting phone calls from someone on behalf of their grandchild, or claiming to actually be their grandchild. The caller says the grandchild has been arrested and urgently needs money to get out of jail. Sometimes another person gets on the phone pretending to be an attorney or law enforcement officer, demanding bond money be sent immediately through cash apps, gift cards, wire transfers, or even cash pickups. Two victims were struck by a 2001 Toyota Camry Solara traveling west on Government Avenue SW. The vehicle was driven by 78-year-old Donald Gene Bledsole of Hickory. Catawba County EMS and Hickory Fire Department rendered lifesaving aid to the victims, a juvenile and a 40-year-old male. The 40-year-old male suffered serious injuries. He was transported to an…

Local Authorities Warn Of Scam Involving Pet Photos

According to the Caldwell County Sheriff’s Office, scammers are increasingly targeting people looking for pets online, especially puppies, by asking for a deposit up front, before the victim even sees the pet in person, then disappearing once the money is sent. Here’s what usually happens: Scammers post pet photos online, like Facebook, Craigslist, Instagram, or fake breeder websites. They claim the pet is in high demand and that multiple families are interested. They ask for a deposit to hold the pet, usually through Cash App, Zelle, Venmo, Apple Pay, or gift cards. After the deposit is sent, they stop responding and…

Inmate Death In Burke County Under Investigation

The Burke County Sheriff’s Office reports the death of an inmate. On January 18, 2026, an inmate was found unresponsive in his cell at the Burke County Jail after another detainee alerted detention staff. Detention and medical personnel responded immediately and initiated life-saving measures while requesting assistance from Burke County EMS, who also responded to the scene. The inmate was transported to an area medical facility for medical treatment. After several weeks of medical care, he passed away on February 11. The Burke County Sheriff’s Office has requested that the North Carolina State Bureau of Investigation (SBI) conduct an independent investigation.…

Kvapil Wins ASA STARS National Tour at Hickory Motor Speedway

It was only fitting that the Tar Heel 250 presented by Appalachian Sucker Punch came down to two drivers from the state of North Carolina at Hickory Motor Speedway. When the dust settled, Caden Kvapil (Mooresville, NC) won for the first time in an epic late race battle with Brown. Carson Brown (New London, NC) was seeking to become the first driver in ASA STARS National Tour history to win three consecutive races. Caldwell Community College Softball Suffers First Loss at NJCAA World Series

Caldwell Community College & Technical Institute’s softball team fell to defending national champion North Dakota State College of Science 3-1 Thursday evening in a winner’s bracket match-up between the top two seeds of the National Junior College Athletic Association Division-Three World Series in DeWitt, NY. Chloe Wilson put Caldwell on the board in the top of the second inning with a solo homerun. Lenoir-Rhyne Men’s Lacrosse Have Four All-Americans

The Lenoir-Rhyne men’s lacrosse team saw four individuals named to the United State Intercollegiate Lacrosse Association (USILA) All-America team. Donovan Powell (Attack) was named to the first team, Matthew Mancini (Face-off) to the second team, and Mac Murphy (Defense) and John Mink (Long Stick Midfielder) were honorable mention honorees. Powell is Lenoir-Rhyne’s lone first team honoree. The senior had a historic season, finishing with 88 points (53g, 35a), the second most in program history and tied for the sixth most in the country this year. NASCAR Driver Kyle Busch Passes Away

Former NASCAR Cup Series Champion Kyle Busch passed away earlier today (Thursday). Busch was scheduled to race in the Legends series at Hickory Motor Speedway on Thursday as part of the ASA Stars National Tour. Instead, WHKY learned Thursday morning that he had fallen ill as he was unable to have a scheduled call in on WHKY’s On Second Thought program. Fox Sports had reported that he had be hospitalized by Thursday afternoon. ASA STARS National Tour Races at Hickory Motor Speedway Tonight, Tri-County on Saturday

The Appalachian Sucker Punch ASA STARS National Tour returns to North Carolina for a massive doubleheader weekend featuring two 250-lap Super Late Model events at two of the region’s premier short tracks. The action begins Thursday, May 21 at Hickory Motor Speedway with the Tar Heel 250 before moving just 30 minutes away to Tri County Speedway on Saturday, May 23 for the Carolina 250. McDougall was the third best finisher who didn’t advance with a team, coming one spot shy of qualifying for the national championships. Gage Peterson delivered another fabulous start for App State (29-19, 16-10). The right-hander went seven innings, allowing just two runs on six hits and struck out six to improve to 8-0 on the season. Hickory Crawdads Games Canceled This Weekend

Appalachian State Tennis Courts Dedicated to Jim and Elaine Jones

Appalachian State University officially dedicated the new App State Tennis Courts as the Jim & Elaine Jones Outdoor Tennis Complex on May 1, honoring the deep and lasting legacy of a couple who have spent decades serving the institution and the Boone community. As Director of Athletics from 1974-80, Jim Jones was a transformative leader, expanding the athletic department from six to 22 sports and trailblazing the university’s first scholarships for female student-athletes. Beyond his administrative success, Jones was a legendary figure on the court. Over a 20-year coaching career, he amassed a 267-86 record, secured eight conference championships and earned inductions into…

NCHSAA Announces Sanctioning of Girls Flag Football, Boys Volleyball

The Board of Directors of the North Carolina High School Athletic Association voted on Wednesday to officially sanction Women’s Flag Football as an NCHSAA championship sport, marking a historic step in expanding athletic opportunities for female student-athletes across the state. The decision comes after several years of growing interest and participation in the sport at the high school level, as well as successful programs across the state and increased national momentum for women’s flag football. High School Flag Football in North Carolina will now be governed by the NCHSAA and will compete under NFHS rules. The season will take place in…

Junior Eve Neild put together a strong opening round at the NCAA South Regional on Monday, carding a 75 (+3) to tie for 18th out of 96 golfers. Lenoir-Rhyne University Athletics has announced the hiring of Steve Peeler as Athletic Field & Campus Enhancement Specialist, bringing one of the most respected names in professional turf and field management to campus. Peeler, a Morganton, N.C., native, joins Lenoir-Rhyne following a distinguished career spanning more than three decades in Major League Baseball, where he served as a senior groundskeeper and field manager at the sport’s highest level. Lenoir-Rhyne Men’s Lacrosse Ends Regular Season With Win

The Lenoir-Rhyne men’s lacrosse team closed out their regular season on Tuesday with a convincing 19-5 win over visiting Coker. Donovan Powell (3g, 3a), Owen Scott (5g, 1a), and Jarrett Huff (4g, 2a) led the team with six points each. The Bears finish their regular season slate with a 10-3 (7-2 SAC) record and will host a SAC quarterfinal game on Saturday at Moretz. Time and opponent will be determined later this week. Jarrett Huff scored the opener just over a minute in. Coker held Lenoir-Rhyne scoreless for over nine minutes of play before the Bears scored five goals in four…

Lenoir-Rhyne Baseball Wins Non-Conference Matchup

The Lenoir-Rhyne baseball team took down Ferrum 12-1 in a seven inning, non-conference game on Tuesday afternoon. The Bears jumped out to a 10-0 lead after three and cruised to the finish. Mackenzie Wainwright was 3-for-4 with two runs scored while Conor Linkfield was 3-for-4 with an RBI and two runs scored. Mackenzie Wainwright opened the game with at triple and scored on a throwing error by the catcher. With the bases loaded, Drew Beard hit a 2 RBI single. App State Women’s Golf in Second at Sun Belt Championship After Round Two

Sixth-seeded App State Women’s Golf will enter Wednesday’s final round of stroke play at the Sun Belt Championship tournament in a tie for second place among 13 teams. After 36 of 54 holes in the stroke play portion of the event, the Mountaineers are seven shots behind first-place ULM, which is 4 over. App State and Texas State are both 11 over, seven shots ahead of the tie for fourth and fifth places between Old Dominion and Coastal Carolina. Appalachian State Baseball’s DiRito Named Sun Belt Pitcher of the Week

App State right-hander Nick DiRito has been named the Sun Belt Pitcher of the Week, following a dominant performance in the Mountaineers’ victory over James Madison on April 17. The redshirt junior fired a career-high eight scoreless innings and matched a personal best with eight strikeouts to earn the victory in a 10-0 tone-setting win for App State in the series opener. DiRito allowed just three baserunners in the ballgame – a walk and two hits – and faced just two over the minimum. Lenoir-Rhyne Men’s Golf Finish Eighth at SAC Championship

The Lenoir-Rhyne men’s golf team tied for eighth at the SAC Championships on Sunday/Monday. The Bears scored rounds of 312-303-309 to finish with a 924 (+60). #5 Wingate was the top team finisher thanks to an 856 (-8). Lincoln Memorial’s Harry Dickins won the individual title with a 206 (-10). Wingate, Lincoln Memorial, Catawba, and Anderson all advance to tomorrow’s match play semi-finals. Juniors Matthew McDougall and Slade Aliff led the Bears, each tying for 23rd with a 228 (+12). Both shot at least one round of 73, the lowest round of any Bear at the championships. Lenoir-Rhyne Softball Sweeps Doubleheader Over Lees-McRae

The Lenoir-Rhyne softball team combined for 39 hits and 10 home runs in 16-6 and 16-8 wins over Lees-McRae on Monday. Avery Kiger led the way at the plate, going 5-for-7 with four RBIs, two home runs, and two runs scored. Appalachian State Track and Field Teams Compete In Multiple Events

Matthew Gray and Thomas Wlazlowski broke school records to lead the App State men’s and women’s track and field teams at the Wake Forest Invitational this past weekend. The Mountaineers also sent athletes to the Aggie Invitational. Gray continued his dominant pole vault season, eclipsing his previous school record, which he set three weeks ago at the Raleigh Relays. At Wake Forest, he cleared a bar of 5.61m/18-4.75, catapulting him to eighth in the NCAA this outdoor season. Lenoir-Rhyne Women’s Soccer Elevates Zador To Associate Head Coach

Holly Zador, who will be starting her second year with the Lenoir-Rhyne women’s soccer program in the fall, has been promoted to Associate Head Coach, as announced by head coach Justin Maullin on Monday. “Holly has been a great addition to our staff and program and was a huge part of our success this past season,” said Maullin. “She is a hard worker, an excellent coach, tireless recruiter, and great with our players and families.” This past season Zador helped lead the Bears to their third Southeast Regional Title in five years, finishing with a record of 13-5-3 (7-1-2 SAC). The Lenoir-Rhyne Track & Field teams split between three locations this week in their final meets before the SAC Championships. Hickory Crawdads Rally to Win Over Salem

The Hickory Crawdads scored seven times in the eighth inning to erase a five-run deficit Saturday night at LP Frans Stadium, defeating the Salem RidgeYaks 13–7. Enddy Azocar set the tone early for Salem, hitting home runs in back-to-back innings to push the Red Sox to a 6–1 lead by the fourth. Lenoir-Rhyne Women’s Tennis Falls in First Ever South Atlantic Conference Championship

App State Softball Downs James Madison

With the score tied at one after Madison Fletcher plated a run with a sacrifice fly in the fourth, Macy Hamby stepped to the plate with the bases loaded and nobody out in the fifth, looking to do damage. West Caldwell Names Saffos as New Head Football Coach

West Caldwell High School is proud to announce the appointment of George Saffos as the new Head Coach of Warrior Football, effective April 14, 2026. Coach Saffos brings over two decades of coaching excellence, championship experience, and a deep-rooted commitment to North Carolina athletics to the Warrior family. Caldwell Community College Softball Sweeps Doubleheader

The Caldwell Community College softball team swept Surry Community College in a doubleheader, winning game one 12-2 and game two, 10-1. In game one, Caldwell had six extra-base hits in a 12-2 mercy rule victory over Surry in game one of a doubleheader Tuesday in Hudson. Chloe Wilson was 2-for-3 with a double, a homerun, and three RBI. Parker Boggs, Lexy Church, and Ally Greer each had two RBI. Lenoir-Rhyne’s Moretz Stadium to Host High School State Lacrosse Championships

The North Carolina High School Athletic Association announced on Tuesday the locations for the 2026 Spring Sports Championships across the state. The Lacrosse State Championships will be held May 29–30 at Lenoir-Rhyne University’s Moretz Stadium. Games will be played Friday at 11:00 a.m., 2:00 p.m., 5:00 p.m., and 8:00 p.m., and Saturday at 10:00 a.m. and 1:00 p.m. More details, including times and classification pairings will be announced later. {Information provided by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association}

Charlotte Hornets to Compete in Play-In Game Tonight

The Charlotte Hornets have qualified for the post-season in the NBA. It’s been so long, they’ve only done this version of it twice, in the first two years of this extended playoff period. Six years ago, the NBA created the “play-in games” to determine the final four playoff spots. The number seven and eight seeded teams play each other, with the number nine and ten seeds squaring off against each other. For the Hornets this season, they are the number nine seeded team. If they lose Tuesday at home to the 10-seeded Miami Heat, the season is over. Win, and they…

App State Men’s Golf traded the lead during a back-and-forth final hour of the Irish Creek Intercollegiate on Tuesday, but Catawba pulled ahead one last time and won the team title by two strokes over the Mountaineers. This home run set the Mountaineers’ new single-season home run record, surpassing Katie Boyd’s record of 17 set in 2009. In his first attempt at the 400 this outdoor season, the Leicester, N.C., native ran an impressive time of 46.64 to earn his first career weekly award from the conference. Merhtens, a shortstop and second baseman, has delivered one of the most productive seasons in program history during the 2026 campaign. LR Women’s Basketball Head Coach Named WBCA Thirty Under 30

Head women’s basketball coach Emry Tsitouris has been named a Women’s Basketball Coaches Association (WBCA) Thirty Under 30 honoree. The WBCA Thirty Under 30 program was created to recognize 30 up-and-coming women’s basketball coaches aged 30 and under at all levels of the game. Each honoree has exemplified their involvement in community service, mentorship and impact on others, professional manner, and attitude and professional association involvement. In just her first season at the helm coach Tsitouris led the Bears to one of the best seasons in program history, finishing with a 26-6 (20-2 SAC) record, winning the SAC Regular Season Championship,…

Appalachian State Football Changes South Alabama Contest Date

App State Football’s post-Thanksgiving road game at South Alabama to close the 2026 regular season has shifted to Friday, Nov. 27 in Mobile, Ala. The game is tentatively scheduled for 3 p.m. ET (2 p.m. CT) on ESPN+. A full 2026 schedule for the Mountaineers was announced earlier this month, with the regular season finale originally scheduled for Nov. 28. Two of App State’s four Sun Belt Conference road games are now scheduled for Fridays, with the Mountaineers playing at Coastal Carolina on Oct. 16. App State Hires Bessey Who Returns to the Area

App State has hired Aaron Bessey as a Senior Associate Athletics Director for External Operations, Director of Athletics Doug Gillin announced Tuesday. Bessey, who most recently served as Athletic Director at Division II Young Harris (Ga.) College, served in external relations roles at UNC Asheville, Lenoir-Rhyne and Georgia State after starting his professional career in marketing with a two-year stint at App State. Trio of LR Men’s Basketball Players Earn Academic All-District

Lenoir-Rhyne men’s basketball placed three individuals on the College Sport Communicators Academic All District Team. Honorees are Sam Martin, Junior Hodnett, and Tyler Burraston. The 2025–26 Academic All-District men’s basketball teams, chosen by College Sports Communicators, honor top student-athletes for excellence in both academics and athletics across all collegiate divisions. To qualify, athletes must have a minimum GPA of 3.5 and either have started in 66% or participated in 90% of their games during the season. Mancini Named South Atlantic Conference Men’s Lacrosse Specialist of the Week

The South Atlantic Conference has announced its WePlayed Sports Men’s Lacrosse Players of the Week for games played during week seven of the 2026 season. Lenoir-Rhyne’s Matthew Mancini was named the Specialist of the Week. Mancini, a senior from Middletown, Md., was dominant at the faceoff X for the Bears who defeated Catawba and Lincoln Memorial last week. Mancini won 31-of-35 faceoffs for the week, earning a 88.6 success rate. He scooped up 15 ground balls and added a pair of assists. He was 19-of-22 against Catawba and 12-of-13 against LMU. App State Baseball Wins Series Over Old Dominion

Wilson, the grad transfer from DII Belmont Abbey, earned his first win of the season, while Fisher, a five-year Mountaineer, earned his seventh career save, the 11th-most in program history. Lenoir-Rhyne Men’s Lacrosse Falls to Wingate in Charlotte

For the second straight year the Lenoir-Rhyne Bears and Wingate Bulldogs met in the Crown Classic, a prestigious lacrosse showcase held in Charlotte. This year the #8 Bulldogs came out on top, defeating the #11 Bears 14-10. The opening quarter was a game of runs as Lenoir-Rhyne scored the first three before Wingate answered with four of their own. Owen Scott stopped the bleeding but a Bulldog goal with six seconds left in the frame gave Wingate a 5-4 lead after one. After the Bears tied it at five, Wingate scored the last four goals of the half to take a…

Lenoir-Rhyne Softball Sweeps Doubleheader Over Catawba

Lenoir-Rhyne swept Catawba 6-5 and 9-1 on the road Saturday. Autumn Joyce hit an RBI single with two outs in the top of the seventh to tie game one at five, then an error by Catawba give LR the 6-5 lead. Madison Wilson shut down Catawba in the bottom of the seventh to give the Bears a SAC opening victory. In the night cap, Avery Kiger hit a grand slam while Lani Warren also went deep as LR completed the sweep with a 9-1 victory. The win extends the Bears winning streak to 14 games. The Lenoir-Rhyne women’s tennis team dominated Mars Hill Saturday afternoon, defeating them 7-0 and improving to 8-1 on the year. Appalachian State Baseball Falls Again to Coastal Carolina

Despite a tremendous start from Gage Peterson, App State Baseball was unable to hold the lead late in a 7-4 loss to No. 16 Coastal Carolina on Saturday. First pitch of the series finale has been moved up to 11 a.m. on Sunday, due to inclement weather expected later in the afternoon. Peterson fired a career-high seven scoreless, striking out nine, while conceding just two hits and two walks. He retired six in a row twice in the ballgame. Peterson lowered his ERA to 1.24 through five starts this season and has now struck out 41 batters in 29.0 innings, while…

App State Softball Falls to Coastal Carolina

Although it scored a first-inning run on a Grace Barrett sacrifice fly, the App State Softball team struggled to find offense in the middle innings, which helped Coastal Carolina post a 3-2 win on Saturday. Coastal Carolina hit a two-run home run in the bottom of the first to take the lead and a crucial insurance home run in the fifth to clinch the victory. App State (20-6, 1-1 SBC) made things interesting in the seventh inning after KB Bradshaw smashed a solo home run to right-center field with two outs. Appalachian State Women’s Tennis Falls to Nationally Ranked North Florida

The App State tennis team fell 4-2 to undefeated No. 72 North Florida on Saturday. Savannah Dada-Mascoll extended her winning streak to 12 matches, her longest since recording 16 consecutive wins in 2024. During this stretch, she has dropped only two sets, the last of which came against UT Arlington on Feb. 28. Freshman Allegra Hodson blanked her opponent in her 6-0, 6-2 victory at No. 4 singles. App State Baseball Falls to Coastal Carolina

App State Baseball scored two runs in the seventh and four in the eighth to erase a six-run deficit, but was unable to get over the hump, in a 10-6 loss to No. 16 Coastal Carolina in Friday’s Sun Belt opener. In a game that was scoreless through five innings of play, there was plenty of drama late, as Riley Luft tied the score at 6-6 with a two-run single in the eighth inning, before Coastal Carolina (11-6, 1-0) regained the advantage with a four-run ninth.
