Lenoir-Rhyne Track and Field Competes at Nationals

Three Bears competed in the NCAA Division II National Championship on Friday, highlighted by Tobechukwu Nwokocha’s runner-up finish in the long jump. Staying in the competition will be Havyn Spain, who advanced to the final in the 200 meter dash. Tobechukwu Nwokocha earned a national runner-up finish in the long jump at the NCAA Division II National Championships on Friday. Appalachian State Softball Defeats Coastal Carolina

After a two-run home run by Coastal Carolina tied the score at three in the bottom of the fourth inning, the App State softball team used a Grace Barrett two-RBI double in the top of the fifth to give it a lead it wouldn’t relinquish, as it scored four runs in the final three innings to post a 7-3 win in its Sun Belt opener on Friday. Lenoir-Rhyne Baseball Opens Series Against Anderson With Shutout

Paul Toovalian spun a gem on Friday night, throwing a complete game two-hitter while striking out eleven as the Bears defeated Anderson 12-0 to open their weekend SAC series. Mackenzie Wainwright ended the game via mercy rule with a three-run bomb in the bottom of the seventh. The home run was Wainwright’s second of the game as he finished the day 3-for-5 with two home runs and six RBIs. Lenoir-Rhyne Women’s Basketball Advances in the NCAA Division ll Tournament

The Lenoir-Rhyne women’s basketball team advanced to the second round of the NCAA Division ll Tournament with an 83-74 victory against Augusta. Amiya Porter and Aaliyah El combined for over half of the Bears points while Trista Charles scored a season-high 19 points and added three rebounds. The Bears will face-off against #3 Coker in the second round of the tournament tomorrow evening at 5:00 PM. Appalachian State Football Announces 2026 Schedule

The Sun Belt Conference on Friday announced the complete 2026 regular season schedule for App State Football. The schedule has five Saturday home dates to go along with one midweek home game in league play: a nationally televised Thursday home contest against James Madison on Oct. 22, six days after App State plays a Friday night game at Coastal Carolina. The nonconference schedule is made up of four September games all played in the state of North Carolina, and the Mountaineers’ league schedule starts with six East Division matchups before concluding with a pair of games against West Division foes. Appalachian State Women’s Tennis Edges Lipscomb

The App State tennis team defeated Lipscomb 4-3 in a neutral-site thriller Friday on North Florida’s campus, coming out on top in a season defined by winner-take-all sets. Friday’s match marked the fifth time this season the outcome came down to the final set of singles play with the match score tied 3-3. After dropping all four of those previous occurrences, the Mountaineers (6-7) turned the tide through Sreenidhi Balaji, who clinched the victory in a three-set nail-biter to secure her fourth consecutive win. Eighth-ranked Savannah Dada-Mascoll cruised to her 11th straight win, recording her second bagel of the season in…

CVCC’s Mills Named All-Region Second Team

One member of the the Catawba Valley Community College men’s basketball program has been honored by Region 10 for his performance on the court following the completion of the 2025-26 season. Red Hawks sophomore wing TJay Mills was named Second Team All-Region. The Winston-Salem native led CVCC with 16.5 points and 1.5 steals per contest this past winter. He also ranked third in assists per game (2.7). The Red Hawks finished their 2025-26 season with a 20-11 record. It was the seventh time in program history that the CVCC men’s basketball team had won 20 or more games in a season…

Maiden Wins the State 4A Girls Basketball Championship

The Maiden High School girls basketball team has won the state 4A championship at the Lawrence Joel Veterans Memorial Coliseum on the campus of Wake Forest University in Winston-Salem. The Blue Devils downed Fairmont, 44-39. Maiden went undefeated on the season (30-0) and wins their first ever state championship. Maiden’s Khiara Culliver (21 points, 11 rebounds) was named the Most Valuable Player. Box score available here.

The Lenoir-Rhyne women’s lacrosse team dropped at 17-6 contest at #6 Embry-Riddle on Wednesday. Kirra Olson led the Bears with three goals, her 18th career hat trick. Embry-Riddle scored four consecutive goal to open the game before an unassisted score from Kirra Olson stopped the bleeding. The Eagles led 7-2 after one. There were just four combined goals in the second quarter and Embry-Riddle went into the break ahead 9-2. Seven CVCC Women’s Basketball Players Earn All-Region Honors

Seven members of the Catawba Valley Community College women’s basketball team have been honored by Region 10 following the competition of the 2025-26 regular season. The Red Hawks tied a program record for their most Region 10 Academic All-Region selections with seven. In order to be eligible for Region 10 Academic All-Region, a student athlete must have a 3.7 GPA after their first semester or a 3.6 GPA with two or more semesters. Among those CVCC women’s basketball players who were honored include Charlotte Coyer, Addison Sisk, Kameron Smith, Jordyn Adams, Durajah Hardin, Jonnah Castillo and Alexander Castillo. Luczynski Adds All-Region Honors to His Honors

Lenoir-Rhyne men’s basketball junior Conrad Luczynski has been named to the D2CCA All-Region Second Team, as voted by the regions’ athletic media personnel. The big man from Bartlett, Illinois, leads the team at 14.1 ppg, 7.7 rpg, and is fourth in the country with 78 blocks. He third in the country with a season field goal percentage of 66.5%. He posted a career-high 27 points on the road at Tusculum earlier this year and leads the team with eight double-doubles. In just two seasons at Lenoir-Rhyne Luczynski ranks fifth all-time with 121 career blocks. He was named to the All-SAC First…

Lenoir-Rhyne’s El Earns All-Region Honors

Aaliyah El has been named to the D2CCA All-Region First Team, as voted on by the regions’ media personnel. The junior from Charlotte, N.C., is the team leader with an average of 17.5 ppg on .515 shooting from the field. She scored in double-figures in all but three games and posted 11 games of 20 or more points. In addition to her offense, El is the team leader with 67 steals. Appalachian State Softball Continues to Win

On the back of a lead-off home run and a third-inning home run by Makayla McClain, the App State softball team sprinted out to a 10-0 victory in five innings over the Charleston Southern Buccaneers on Wednesday. The win was App State’s sixth in a row, with all six coming via run rule. CCC&TI’s Cheek Named NJCAA Division 3 Softball Pitcher of the Week

Caldwell Community College & Technical Institute student-athlete Zoe Cheek (Rhonda, NC-East Wilkes HS) earned national recognition on Wednesday as the National Junior College Athletic Association (NJCAA) Division III Softball Pitcher of the Week for the week of March 2–8. Cheek turned in a dominant week in the circle, posting a 3–0 record in three starts while pitching 19.0 innings. She allowed 19 hits and five runs, with only one earned run, while recording 11 strikeouts and issuing no walks. Her performance resulted in an impressive 0.47 earned run average (ERA) for the week. Appalachian State Men’s Golf Finish Ninth at Surf Club Invitational

Aidan Browning led App State Men’s Golf at the Surf Club Invitational, shooting a 6-under 65 in the final round and tying for 14th place with a 5-under total of 208. As hosts of the event, the Mountaineers placed ninth in the 18-team field with a 4-under total (291-279-278—848). App State Softball Downs College of Charleston

In the team’s first midweek competition of the season, the App State softball team exploded for nine runs in the first inning, which helped shoot it to a 12-2 victory in five innings. App State’s nine runs in the first inning marked the most it has scored in a single inning this season. The victory marked App State’s (18-5) fifth victory in a row, with each of them coming via run rule. The Mountaineers have scored 59 runs over their last five games. Appalachian State Women’s Golf Finishes Second at Low Country Intercollegiate

App State Women’s Golf posted another strong showing, placing second in a 15-team field Tuesday at the Low Country Intercollegiate. Ona Lukes tied for fourth place as one of three Mountaineers to post a top-15 result, carding an 8-over total (77-73-74—224) on the par-72 layout. Zoe Duval finished 14th at 12 over (77-77-74—228), and Alisa Pressley tied for 15th at 14 over (82-73-75—230). Lenoir-Rhyne Women’s Golf Competes at Jerry Surratt Memorial Intercollegiate

Lenoir-Rhyne women’s golf took 13th at the 18-team Jerry Surratt Memorial Intercollegiate this week. 13 teams competing were ranked in the top-50, including #1 Wingate, #2 Dallas Baptist, #3 Anderson, #4 Findlay, #5 Grand Valley State, #7 North Georgia, and #9 Lander. 48 of the 96 golfers were top-150 players in Division II. App’s Schafer Named Sun Belt Softball Player of the Week

App State Softball freshman Taylor Schafer has been named the Sun Belt Player of the Week after tallying a four-homer, 14-RBI weekend during App State’s sweep of the Mountaineer Classic. Schafer’s standout performance included two grand slams, one against Furman on Friday and the other against Queens on Sunday. She became the second Mountaineer this season to hit multiple grand slams, and gives App State five grand slams on the season. Lenoir-Rhyne Finan Wins South Atlantic Conference Men’s Golfer of the Week

Lenoir-Rhyne’s Sean Finan was named the South Atlantic Conference men’s golfer of the week. Finan won the Spring Bulldog Clash in Pawley’s Island, SC this past week. Finan shot back-to-back rounds of 70 followed by a 69 in the third and final round for a tournament score 209 (-1). In the tournament, he totaled 10 birdies and never shot above par. In the 102 player field, he was the only player to finish the tournament under par, winning by two strokes. In par-4 scoring he was tied for the lead (3.87, -4) and in par 5 scoring (4.67, -3). The NJCAA Region 10 Division One Men’s Basketball All-Region teams were announced on Monday. Jaylen Golston and Jared Taylor from Caldwell Community College & Technical Institute were named All-Region First Team. Golston, a sophomore forward from Columbia, SC, averaged 15.9 points per game and 8.2 rebounds per game. Lenoir-Rhyne Women Pick Up Two Seed, Men Four Seed in NCAA Basketball Regionals

Head coach Emry Tsitouris and the Lenoir-Rhyne (24-5, 20-2 SAC) women’s basketball team have qualified for the eighth NCAA Tournament in program history, earning the #2 seed in the Southeast Region. The Bears will face #7 seed Augusta on Friday in the opening round. The entire NCAA Southeast Regional will be hosted in Harrogate, Tennessee, on the campus of #1 seed Lincoln Memorial University. The four quarterfinals will take place on Friday, the two semi-finals on Saturday, and the Southeast Region championship on Monday night. Hickory Motor Speedway Results From Saturday, 3/7/26

Winners from opening night at Hickory Motor Speedway Saturday night included: Late Models: Michael Bumgarner Limiteds: Graham Hollar Super Trucks: Michael Bumgarner Renegades: Steve Smart Street Stocks: Cody DeMarmels Carolina Pro Late Models: TJ Moon Crown Vics: Todd Bradburry The Pass Pro All Star Series will be at Hickory Motor Speedway Friday, March 13 and Saturday, March 14th for the CorvetteParts.net Easter Bunny 200. The NASCAR Local Racing Series will race again on March 21. Additionally, the track announced that it will receive a full repave of the track sometime in June. The track was last fully repaved in 1992.

App State Wrestling Wins SoCon Championship For Fourth Straight Year

Similar drama. Same satisfying result. With the SoCon Championships tournament being decided in the heavyweight finale for the second straight year, App State Wrestling standout Stephan Monchery came through again to produce a thrilling team title in Kimmel Arena for the second straight year. App State won its fourth straight SoCon tournament title to top the program’s three-peat from 1995-97. Behind a season-high 11 strikeouts from starting pitcher Gage Peterson, App State Baseball clinched a weekend series win with a 13-3 win against Presbyterian in seven innings Saturday. Peterson allowed six hits and three runs (all unearned) over six innings of work, with only two walks. His 11 strikeouts were the most by a Mountaineer pitcher since Xander Hamilton had 15 against Coastal Carolina in 2023. App State Indoor Track and Field Garner 11 All Conference Honors

Eleven Mountaineers collected All-SBC accolades for their performances at the Sun Belt Indoor Track & Field Championships, the league announced Saturday. Senior Ava Studney became the Mountaineers’ first Sun Belt women’s indoor pole vault champion since joining the league in 2014. Studney garnered gold and first-team All-SBC honors after recording a clearance of 4.08m (13′ 4.5″). Her mark moved her to second outright in the App State all-time list. Fellow vaulter, sophomore Lilly Nichols, earned third-team All-SBC honors after collecting bronze with a clearance of 3.88m (12′ 8.75″). Christmas, a senior catcher from Charlotte, N.C., went 8-for-14 over a 4-0 week for the Bears, highlighted by hitting for the cycle in a win over USC Beaufort. Lenoir-Rhyne’s Madison Primm was named the South Atlantic Conference WePlayed Sports Softball Player of the Week for games played during week three of the 2026 season, the conference office announced Tuesday. Primm, a junior infielder from Stem, N.C., went 5-for-11 in the Bears 4-0 week and scored six runs in those four wins. She crushed three home runs and drove in nine runs while walking three times and not striking out. For the week, she hit .455 with a .533 on base percentage and 1.273 slugging percentage. High School Basketball Playoff Brackets Released

The North Carolina High School Athletic Association have released the playoff brackets for the girls and boys tournament beginning Tuesday. For Hickory Metro teams, first round matchups and seeds include: Girls 1A NCSSM-Morganton is a 20 seed and will go to North Stokes in the first round Girls 3A Draughn is a 9 seed and will host Madison Patton is a 23 seed and will travel to Lincolnton in the first round Girls 4A East Burke is a nine seed and will host East Henderson in the first round Bandys is a 12 seed and will host 21 seed Bunker Hill…

App State Softball Splits A Pair of Games at the Tar Heel Invitational

Following a victory to open the day over Princeton, the App State Softball team entered the seventh inning against North Carolina trailing by two runs. Facing their final three outs, the Mountaineers saw an RBI groundout by KB Bradshaw, after Makayla McClain and Julia Girk reached base, to pull within one run, before Taylor Schafer delivered home the game-tying run with two outs to send the game to extras. Despite the seventh-inning fight by App State (9-2), unbeaten North Carolina (13-0) kept the Mountaineers scoreless and hit a walk-off home run to win the game 5-3. App State’s only two losses…

Lenoir-Rhyne Baseball Wins Weekend Opener Over Carson-Newman

Lenoir-Rhyne scored eight runs in the first inning and never looked back, defeating the Carson-Newman Eagles 13-10. Leading that charge was Seth Christmas, who hit two home runs, including a first inning grand slam. In total the Bears finished with 12 hits and nine walks, compared to nine hits and seven walks from Carson-Newman. Michael Burnley (2-0) struck out four in the victory while Jack Renaud (S, 2) picked up the two inning save. Three of Lenoir-Rhyne’s first four batters of the game were walked, setting up Seth Christmas for his first inning grand slam. Following two more walks, Drew Beard…

App State Baseball Opens Weekend With 9-3 Win Over NC A&T

Tanner Nolan and Nick DiRito combined to strike out 13 batters, while the offense blasted three home runs as App State Baseball rolled past North Carolina A&T 9-3 on Friday. The Mountaineers’ 13 strikeouts without a walk are the most since 2016, when App State fanned 14 in nine walkless innings also against North Carolina A&T. Nolan was locked in from the start. The lefty from Deptford, N.J. struck out two batters in each of his first two innings and bounced back after a solo home run in the third. Nolan did not walk a batter. He faced just three over…

Appalachian State Men’s Basketball Edged Out by Marshall

With just 40 seconds to play in regulation and the App State men’s basketball team trailing by three, Kasen Jennings buried a step-back 3-pointer from the top of the key to tie the score at 92, which sent the home crowd into a brief frenzy. On the other end, Marshall’s Landen Joseph calmly stepped into a pull-up jumper from the right elbow to send the Thundering Herd ahead by two with 12 seconds to go. Looking to either tie the score or take the lead, Jalen Tot raced into the lane and nearly missed an and-one chance, but stepped to the…

CCC&TI Softball’s Smith Wins NJCAA Division 3 Player of the Week

Caldwell Community College and Technical Institute softball’s Skylar Smith earned recognition as the National Junior College Athletic Association Softball Division-Three Player of the Week for the Week of February 9-15. In four games, Skylar hit .636 (7 for 11) with a double, a homerun, and nine runs batted in. Caldwell was 3-1 in the four games. In game one of a doubleheader against the Belmont Abbey Developmental squad, a 3-2 loss, Smith had only two at-bats and failed to reach base. However, in game two that the Cobras won 7-3, Smith went two-for-three with a run scored and two RBI. One…

LR Men’s Basketball Blows Out Coker, Holds Second Place in SAC Standings

Lenoir-Rhyne men’s basketball flexed their muscles on Wednesday night, defeating Coker 107-72. The Bears have scored at least 100 points in six game this year, tied for the fourth most in a season in Lenoir-Rhyne history. Head coach Brent Owen now has 50 wins in just 62 games coached in Hickory. Blessin Kimble led the Bears with an efficient 23 point performance, going 9-14 from the field and finishing with seven boards. Tyler Burraston made six threes and finished with 18 points. The Bears saw themselves down 9-8 before an Akili Evans three put them up for good. That three sparked…

Lenoir-Rhyne Women’s Basketball Wins 16th Straight

In the 50 years of Lenoir-Rhyne women’s basketball no team had ever won 16 consecutive games. Head coach Emry Tsitouris and her Lenoir-Rhyne Bears defeated #12 Coker 89-67 to win their 16th straight contest, breaking the record of 15 set during the 1979-80 season. Lenoir-Rhyne outscored Coker 57-29 in the middle two quarters to pull away from this high powered Cobra squad. Abigail Brown led the team with 23 while Ralee Bare scored 17 on 5-6 shooting from the field. The Bears found themselves in a hole quickly as the Cobras jumped out to a 16-4 lead in the game’s first…

Lenoir-Rhyne Softball Sweeps Doubleheader Over Erskine

The Lenoir-Rhyne softball team got back to .500 on Wednesday with 9-1 and 8-0 victories over Erskine. Madison Wilson and Faith Carrigan didn’t allow an earned run in eight total innings while Madison Primm went 3-for-5 with three home runs and seven RBIs. In game one, Lenoir-Rhyne went up 3-0 after one thanks to a home run from Madison Primm an an RBI single from Montana Honeycutt. The Flying Fleet plated one in the top of the second but RBIs from Avery Kiger and Alexandria Gray extended the lead to 5-1. Neither team scored in the third or fourth innings, and Erskine…

Appalachian State Baseball Schedule Altered This Weekend Due to Expected Weather

Due to impending weather, App State Baseball has adjusted its weekend schedule. Appalachian State Women’s Basketball Edged Out by Coastal Carolina

Despite narrowing the deficit to one and outscoring Coastal Carolina, 21-8, in the fourth quarter, the App State women’s basketball team fell short to the Chanticleers, 65-64. “I am really proud of the fight in our group,” said head coach Alaura Sharp. “There were some key possessions that we didn’t capitalize on. There were also some moments we could have put our head down and quit and we chose to keep scrapping. We have all we need to win down the stretch we have to continue to grow up as a team.” Senior Emma Smith and freshman Riley Stoker combined for…

App State Baseball Falls at Duke

App State Baseball freshman Nico Soul blasted a home run off the batter’s eye on the second pitch of the ballgame, but the Mountaineers were unable to hold an early advantage in a 10-5 loss at Duke, Tuesday. App State (3-1) had a number of scoring opportunities but left 13 runners on base, including five in the first three innings, while Duke (4-1) scored each of its first seven runs of the ballgame with two outs. Soul’s home run was the first of his collegiate career. He entered the season ranked as the Sun Belt’s top impact freshman by D1Baseball. The…

Appalachian State Field Hockey Has 14 Named to National Academic Squad

Fourteen members of the App State field hockey team earned academic honors from the National Field Hockey Coaches Association (NFHCA), the organization announced Tuesday. Grace Bramble, Sarah Callery, Samantha Connors, Bridget Donovan, Zoey Emrick, Carlotta Fabian, Hadley Kuzmicky, Emma Livingston, Sarah Perkins, Baylie Phillips, Darby Reyburn, Kate Richardson, Calista Schwartz and Henriette Stegen were named to the National Academic Squad. To qualify, student-athletes must maintain a cumulative GPA of 3.5 or higher. Kuzmicky and Stegen earned the distinction in each of their four years at App State. Five Mountaineers were also named Scholars of Distinction for achieving a GPA of 3.9…

Lenoir-Rhyne Baseball Dominates USC-Beaufort

Down 4-2, the Lenoir-Rhyne baseball team scored 19 runs over the final four innings to grab a 21-6 win over visiting USC Beaufort on Tuesday. The Bears put up 20 hits in this one, led by four from Lleyton Lackey, Matt Procopio, and Seth Christmas. Christmas, Lackey, and Mackenzie Wainwright each went yard for the second time this season. Six Lenoir-Rhyne pitchers were used in this game with Donnie Canchela getting the start and Preston Drebes picking up the win. Nwokocha Earns Second SAC Weekly Award of the Season

The South Atlantic Conference announced its WePlayed Sports Men’s Indoor Track and Field Athletes of the Week for meets contested during the sixth week of competition. Lenoir-Rhyne’s Tobechukwu Nwokocha earned the field honor. Nwokocha, a graduate student jumper from Nigeria, won the long jump at the VMI Winter Classic with a leap of 7.76 meters (25-5.50). His mark is among the top performances in the SAC this season and sits just .02 meters shy of his own school record set earlier this year. 2025-26 SAC Men’s Track and Field Athletes of the Week Field Dec. 9 – Cadence Dolgin, Carson-Newman Jan.…

LR Indoor Track and Field Competes at VMI Winter Classic and the ASIC Sound Invitational

The Lenoir-Rhyne men’s and women’s track & field teams competed at both the VMI Winter Classic and the ASIC Sound Invitational this past weekend, setting multiple school records in the process. Competing in a loaded distance field at the ASIC Sound Invite, senior Lewis Budgen broke Jacob Parkinson’s school record in the indoor 5000m with a time of 14:29.34, bettering Parkinson’s mark from last year by nearly 10 seconds. Breaking her own record in the triple jump was Mackenzie Pringle, who won the event at the VMI Winter Classic with a leap of 12.48m (40-11.50), good enough for the fifth best…

App State Baseball Sweeps Doubleheader Over Canisius

App State Baseball completed a season-opening series sweep Saturday, taking both games of a doubleheader against Canisius by scores of 13-3 (in seven innings) and 8-5 at L.P. Frans Stadium. The Mountaineers, who also started 3-0 as recently as 2023, outscored the Golden Griffins by a 26-10 count over the three games. App State is back in action Tuesday against Duke in Durham. Five-run outbursts in the third and sixth innings of Saturday’s opener, coupled with a two-run bottom of the seventh, enabled that game to end early via the 10-run rule. Appalachian State Women’s Indoor Track and Field Win VMI Winter Classic

The App State women’s track and field team rounded out the indoor regular season with a first-place team finish at the VMI Winter Classic on Saturday. The Mountaineers registered five individual first-place finishes, tallied 31 top five finishes, and scored 209 points on the day. With a leap of 1.65m (5′ 5″), freshman Ashlynn Wimberly placed first in the women’s high jump. She was 0.03m (0′ 1″) shy of matching her personal best of 1.68m (5′ 6″), which she cleared at the VMI Team Challenge on Jan. 17. Lenoir-Rhyne Baseball Rebounds with Doubleheader Sweep of Wingate

The Lenoir-Rhyne Baseball team rebounded from Friday’s night shutout loss to sweep Wingate 5-2 and 3-2 on Saturday, taking the SAC opening series on Saturday. Hugo Tafoya went 6 2/3 innings in the opener to earn his first win while Jack Renaud got the last seven outs for the save. In the night cap, Kellen Gradisar went the distance for the complete game shutout. LR Softball Drop Both Games of a Doubleheader

The Lenoir-Rhyne Softball team dropped a pair of games at USC-Aiken on Saturday. Lani Warren connected on a three-run home run in the night cap but the Bears fell 3-0 and 10-5 on Saturday. Adriana Lamantia collected two hits in each game while Autumn Joyce added three more hits. In game one, Aiken got a single run in the second then plated a pair of runs in the sixth for a 3-0 victory in the opener. Appalachian State Softball Splits A Pair of Games at Spiro Classic

Playing in two tight games to close out the Spiro Classic, the App State softball team took down Columbia 4-2 to open the day before falling to UNCG 6-3 on a walk-off home run in the ninth inning to close the weekend. Despite the outcome of the second game, the Mountaineers still closed play in Greensboro with a 4-1 record. App State’s (8-1) eight-game winning streak ended after the loss to UNCG (4-5), but it still made history as the second-longest winning streak in program history, trailing only a nine-game winning streak in 2021. CVCC Men’s Basketball Wins Eighth in a Row

The Catawba Valley Community College men’s basketball team won its eighth-straight game on Saturday afternoon, defeating Johnston Community College 98-60 at the Tarlton Complex. KJ Burke led the Red Hawks (15-10, 11-6 in Region 10) with a game-high 19 points, while Caden Caskey added 18 points. Izaiah Littlejohn (13 points), Josh Moore (13) and Trent Floystad (12) also reached double figures in scoring for CVCC. The Red Hawks powered ahead to a 47-33 halftime lead behind 10 first-half 3-pointers, including four from Burke and three from Caskey. App State Women’s Basketball Defeats Georgia State in Overtime

The App State women’s basketball team snapped a three-game drought with a 73-67 overtime victory over Georgia State for Pink Day and Alumni Day on Saturday. With 0:23 left in regulation, senior Elena Pericic drained a crucial 3-point bucket to force overtime, where the Mountaineers outscored the Panthers, 13-7, to seal the win. “I’m obviously really proud of our team,” said head coach Alaura Sharp. “I told them after Wednesday’s game, because we’ve had a rough stretch, our next few days will say a lot about really the type of character that we have. Appalachian State Men’s Basketball Win Streak Snapped at Seven

The App State men’s basketball team’s season-long seven-game winning streak ended on Saturday, as it fell 69-58 to James Madison in Harrisonburg. App State’s sluggish start and a 12-2 second-half JMU run helped the Dukes close out a win on their home floor. Despite the outcome, the Mountaineers (18-10, 10-5 SBC) saw a huge performance from Luke Wilson, who posted 18 points and a career-high 16 rebounds. Wilson helped steady App State in the first half with nine points and nine rebounds, which helped keep App State in the game early when James Madison (14-13, 6-8 SBC) threatened to pull away…

Lenoir-Rhyne Men’s Lacrosse Pulls Out Overtime Win

The Lenoir-Rhyne men’s lacrosse team picked up their first win of the season on Saturday afternoon, defeating Rollins 13-12 in overtime. Owen Scott’s lone goal proved to be the difference in the game as he buried a shot from 10 yards out in overtime for the win. Donovan Powell finished the game with five points coming from four goals and an assist. After going up 2-1 thanks to two Donovan Powell goals, Lenoir-Rhyne saw themselves down 4-2 midway through the second quarter. Appalachian State Women’s Tennis Falls to Mercer

The App State women’s tennis team fell in a 4-3 thriller to Mercer on Saturday. Despite conceding the doubles point early, the Mountaineers rallied to tie the match at 3-3. The contest ultimately came down to a tiebreaking set on Court 2, where Mercer narrowly prevailed to clinch the victory. The Mountaineers began their second stop in Georgia by dropping matches on Courts 2 and 3 in doubles play. Appalachian State Wrestling Stays Undefeated in Southern Conference Duals

Remaining unbeaten in SoCon duals, App State Wrestling started its final road swing of the regular season by taking nine of 10 matches in a 38-3 victory at Davidson on Friday night. The first-place Mountaineers (9-5, 6-0) wrestle again Sunday at preseason favorite Chattanooga, which is 8-5 overall and 5-1 in SoCon duals, with the lone league setback being a 19-18 loss to The Citadel in a showdown decided by tiebreaking criteria. Lenoir-Rhyne Softball Splits Doubleheader at Augusta

The Lenoir-Rhyne softball team split with Augusta on Friday, winning the opener 7-5 in extras before falling 4-2 in the nightcap. Montana Honeycutt and Kynlee Dextraze both went yard for the first time in their career while Lani Warren hit her first home run of the season. Dextraze’s home run was the first at bat of her collegiate career. App State Baseball Opens Season With Win at L.P. Frans Stadium

Kameron Miller took a hit, then delivered a game-changing one. Struck hard in the side by a fouled-off liner toward the home dugout while he was standing in the on-deck circle Friday, Miller went to the plate moments later and delivered a two-run single to break a sixth-inning tie as App State won 5-2 against Canisius in the 2026 season opener at L.P. Lenoir-Rhyne Baseball Suffers First Loss of Season

Lenoir-Rhyne baseball opened South Atlantic Conference play with a 14-0 loss to Wingate on Friday. Wingate’s Chad Harvey pitched a complete game shutout for the Bulldogs, striking out six Bears in the process. Wingate scored four runs in the first on three hits and two errors. Four walks in the second inning plated three more Bulldogs. Three runs in the sixth and seventh innings sealed the win for the visitors. Appalachian State Softball Still Remains Perfect

Makayla McClain delivered a day for the ages on Friday in Greensboro by hitting two grand slams in the first three innings of App State Softball’s contest against Columbia, which helped lead to a 12-0 win in five innings. App State then wrapped up the day by run-ruling Fairfield to sweep the doubleheader and move to 7-0 on the season. To put into context what McClain did in the first game of the doubleheader, only 13 MLB players have ever hit two grand slams in a game. App State Women’s Tennis Wins Third Straight

The App State tennis team took down Kennesaw State 5-2 on the road Friday, securing its third consecutive win. The Mountaineers (3-2) rallied after conceding the doubles point early in the match. WHKY Radio is available on both 1290 AM and 102.3 FM

WHKY TalkRadio broadcast on both 1290 AM and on 102.3 FM to fully cover The Greater Hickory Metro area.

The same programming heard on 1290 AM can also be heard at 102.3 FM. The programming is exactly the same on both frequencies.
